doc.title: Sale with a repurchase agreement — a loan in a trench coat
doc.event: Vane Co. transfers inventory to a financier for $100,000 and agrees to repurchase it in one year for $110,000. Prepare all entries over the year.

variants.label: Forward/call to repurchase at MORE than the sale price

entries.caption: Receive 100,000 with an obligation to repurchase at 110,000

lines.label: the 'sale' proceeds
lines.why: Real cash — but borrowed, not earned.
lines.trap: Repurchase at ≥ the sale price + the right/obligation to buy back = the customer never controlled the asset. No sale exists.

lines.label: a borrowing
lines.why: Substance over form: this is a collateralized loan with inventory as the collateral.
lines.trap: NO Sales Revenue, NO COGS, and the inventory NEVER leaves the seller's balance sheet.

entries.caption: Over the year — the 10,000 spread accretes as interest

lines.label: repurchase price − sale price
lines.why: The cost of using 100,000 for a year is the 10,000 you'll pay above what you received — interest by any honest name.
lines.trap: The spread is never 'loss on repurchase' — it was the price of the money from day one.

lines.label: accretes to 110,000
lines.why: The liability grows to the full repurchase price by the buyback date.
lines.trap: Multi-year deals accrete period by period (effective-interest style), not all at the end.

lines.label: full accreted balance
lines.trap: The inventory needs no re-recognition — it never left.

lines.label: buyback price
lines.trap: Compare: repurchase at LESS than the sale price with a customer PUT option can be a lease or a sale-with-return instead — the price relationship decides the animal.

memorize.body: Repurchase at ≥ original price (forward or call) → FINANCING: cash in = liability; spread = interest expense; inventory stays put. No revenue at any point.

BILL-AND-HOLD is the opposite twist: revenue CAN be recognized without shipping if (1) a substantive reason exists (customer asked), (2) goods are identified separately, (3) ready for transfer, (4) seller can't use or redirect them. Control transfers on paper.

questions.prompt: Inventory is 'sold' for 100,000 with an agreement to repurchase it in one year for 110,000. Sales revenue recognized =

distractors.feedback: Control never transferred — the buyback obligation makes this a FINANCING. Cash 100,000 = a liability; the 10,000 spread = interest expense; the inventory never leaves the books.

build.scaffold: Name the substance first: buyback at a higher price = a loan. Then the three beats are ordinary borrowing mechanics — receive, accrete interest, repay. All three sale-flavored accounts in the bank are bait.
